question_text:
Propose a multi-step synthesis route for the target molecule.

Use the structured benchmark context below as the full task specification.
Return the best chemistry answer grounded only in this context.

<benchmark_context>
{
  "level": "C",
  "task_family": "Multi-Step Synthesis Planning",
  "task_subtype": "reference_route_planning",
  "difficulty": "hard",
  "coverage_tags": [
    "route_depth",
    "branch_handling",
    "convergence",
    "reference_route"
  ],
  "input": {
    "target": "COC(=O)C1(c2ccc(NC(=O)c3nc(C#N)cn3COCC[Si](C)(C)C)c(C3=CCCCC3)c2)CCOCC1"
  }
}
</benchmark_context>
student_answer: Step 1: 1-bromo-4-iodo-2-nitrobenzene undergoes Suzuki coupling with cyclohex-1-en-1-ylboronic acid to give 1-bromo-2-nitro-4-(cyclohex-1-en-1-yl)benzene | Step 2: Br/Li exchange on this aryl bromide, then addition to methyl tetrahydro-4-oxo-2H-pyran-4-carboxylate gives the tertiary carbinol; reductive deoxygenation furnishes methyl 4-(2-nitro-4-(cyclohex-1-en-1-yl)phenyl)tetrahydro-2H-pyran-4-carboxylate | Step 3: Reduce the nitro group (H2/Pd-C or Fe/HCl) to obtain methyl 4-(2-amino-4-(cyclohex-1-en-1-yl)phenyl)tetrahydro-2H-pyran-4-carboxylate | Step 4: From 4-(hydroxymethyl)-2-cyanopyrimine-5-carboxylic acid, perform O-alkylation with BrCH2CH2Si(Me)2tBu to give 4-((2-(tert-butyldimethylsilyl)ethyl)oxymethyl)-2-cyanopyrimine-5-carboxylic acid, then convert it to the corresponding acid chloride with SOCl2 (or oxalyl chloride) | Step 5: Acylate the aniline from Step 3 with this pyrimidine-5-carbonyl chloride to give COC(=O)C1(c2ccc(NC(=O)c3nc(C#N)cn3COCC[Si](C)(C)C)c(C3=CCCCC3)c2)CCOCC1
rescue_original_error: OpenShell worker failed with exit_code=124: unknown error

student_answer: Step 1: 3,4-dichlorobenzaldehyde → 2-(3,4-dichlorophenyl)oxirane (e.g., methylenation to 3,4-dichlorostyrene, then epoxidation with mCPBA) | Step 2: Ethyl 3-bromo-2-cyanothiophene-4-carboxylate → C-5 lithiated intermediate (n-BuLi/LDA), then add 2-(3,4-dichlorophenyl)oxirane to give ethyl 3-bromo-2-cyano-5-[CH(3,4-dichlorophenyl)CH2CH2OH]thiophene-4-carboxylate | Step 3: Pd-catalyzed amination of the 3-bromide with morpholine → ethyl 2-cyano-3-(morpholino)-5-[CH(3,4-dichlorophenyl)CH2CH2OH]thiophene-4-carboxylate | Step 4: Hydrolyze the ethyl ester (NaOH/H2O, then acid workup) to N#Cc1c(N2CCOCC2)sc(C(=O)O)c1C(CCO)c1ccc(Cl)c(Cl)c1

question_text:
Propose a multi-step synthesis route for the target molecule.

Use the structured benchmark context below as the full task specification.

<benchmark_context>
{

    "route_depth",

    "reference_route"

  "input": {
    "target": "N#CCC1CCC2(CC1)OCCO2"
  }
}
</benchmark_context>
student_answer: Step 1: Mono-ketalize 1,4-cyclohexanedione with ethylene glycol (cat. p-TsOH, Dean–Stark) to give the spiro-monoketone intermediate O=C1CCC2(CC1)OCCO2 | Step 2: Olefinate the remaining ketone with cyanomethylene Wittig reagent (Ph3P=CHCN, from bromoacetonitrile/PPh3/base) to form the exocyclic alkenyl nitrile N#CCH=C1CCC2(CC1)OCCO2 | Step 3: Hydrogenate the C=C bond (H2, Pd/C) to furnish the target N#CCC1CCC2(CC1)OCCO2

<benchmark_context>

  "input": {
    "target": "CCOC(=O)c1cccc(Sc2c(Cl)n(CC(=O)N3CC4(CCC4)c4ccccc43)c3c(F)c(Cl)ccc23)c1F"
  }
}
</benchmark_context>
student_answer: Step 1: Convert ethyl 2-fluoro-6-bromobenzoate to ethyl 2-fluoro-6-mercaptobenzoate (thiourea substitution to the isothiouronium salt, then hydrolysis/base workup to the thiol). | Step 2: React this thiol with the appropriately activated dichloro/fluoro azaaryl core matching the target ring system (the chloroaza-phenanthridine/quinoline-type nucleus bearing the ring N and the two Cl/F substituents) under SNAr or Pd-catalyzed C–S coupling to give the key thioether intermediate, ethyl 2-fluoro-6-(azaarylthio)benzoate with the target azaaryl halogen pattern retained. | Step 3: In parallel, acylate the secondary amine HN3CC4(CCC4)c4ccccc43 with bromoacetyl bromide (or 2-bromoacetyl chloride) to form 2-bromo-N-(3CC4(CCC4)c4ccccc43)acetamide. | Step 4: Alkylate the azaaryl ring nitrogen of the thioether intermediate from Step 2 with the bromoacetamide from Step 3 (e.g., K2CO3 or DIPEA, DMF) to install the N-CH2C(O)N side chain and furnish CCOC(=O)c1cccc(Sc2c(Cl)n(CC(=O)N3CC4(CCC4)c4ccccc43)c3c(F)c(Cl)ccc23)c1F.
rescue_original_error: OpenShell worker failed with exit_code=124: unknown error

question_text:
Propose a multi-step synthesis route for the target molecule.

Use the structured benchmark context below as the full task specification.
Return the best chemistry answer grounded only in this context.

<benchmark_context>
{
  "level": "C",
  "task_family": "Multi-Step Synthesis Planning",
  "task_subtype": "reference_route_planning",
  "difficulty": "medium",
  "coverage_tags": [
    "route_depth",
    "branch_handling",
    "convergence",
    "reference_route"
  ],
  "input": {
    "target": "COC1CCC(CN)(c2cc(F)ccc2F)CC1"
  }
}
</benchmark_context>
student_answer: Step 1: 1,4-cyclohexanedione → protect one carbonyl as the ethylene ketal (mono-ketal), leaving the other ketone free | Step 2: Reduce the free ketone to the alcohol (NaBH4), then methylate it (MeI/Ag2O or Me2SO4/base) to give the methoxy-ketal intermediate | Step 3: Hydrolyze the ketal to regenerate the second carbonyl, affording 4-methoxycyclohexanone (≡ 1-methoxycyclohexan-4-one) | Step 4: Add 2,4-difluorophenylmagnesium bromide (or 2,4-difluorophenyllithium) to that ketone to form the tertiary alcohol 4-(2,4-difluorophenyl)-4-hydroxy-1-methoxycyclohexane | Step 5: Convert the tertiary alcohol to the corresponding tertiary nitrile via SN1-type cyanation (e.g. TMSCN/BF3·Et2O or HCN/Lewis acid) to give 4-cyano-4-(2,4-difluorophenyl)-1-methoxycyclohexane | Step 6: Reduce the nitrile (LiAlH4 or H2/Raney Ni) to the aminomethyl group, furnishing COC1CCC(CN)(c2cc(F)ccc2F)CC1.
rescue_original_error: OpenShell worker failed with exit_code=124: unknown error

question_text:
Propose a multi-step synthesis route for the target molecule.

Use the structured benchmark context below as the full task specification.
Return the best chemistry answer grounded only in this context.

<benchmark_context>
{
  "level": "C",
  "task_family": "Multi-Step Synthesis Planning",
  "task_subtype": "reference_route_planning",
  "difficulty": "hard",
  "coverage_tags": [
    "route_depth",
    "branch_handling",
    "convergence",
    "reference_route"
  ],
  "input": {
    "target": "O=C1CO[C@H]2CCCC[C@@H]2N1C1CCN(C2CCC(COCC(F)F)CC2)CC1"
  }
}
</benchmark_context>
student_answer: Step 1: Cyclize cis-2-aminocyclohexanol with a carbonylating reagent (CDI, triphosgene, or carbonyldiimidazole/base) to give the fused bicyclic oxazolidinone NH core, O=C1CO[C@H]2CCCC[C@@H]2N1. | Step 2: Alkylate this ring NH with N-Boc-3-mesyloxypiperidine (or the corresponding 3-bromopiperidine) under basic SN2 conditions to form the C3-linked piperidine intermediate O=C1CO[C@H]2CCCC[C@@H]2N1C1CCN(Boc)CC1. | Step 3: Remove Boc (TFA or HCl) to obtain the secondary piperidine amine O=C1CO[C@H]2CCCC[C@@H]2N1C1CCNCC1. | Step 4: In parallel, convert 4-(hydroxymethyl)cyclohexanone to 4-(2,2-difluoroethoxymethyl)cyclohexanone by O-alkylation with 2,2-difluoroethyl bromide (or tosylate), giving cyclohexanone-CH2OCH2CF2H. | Step 5: Reductive amination of the Step 3 piperidine with the Step 4 ketone (NaBH(OAc)3 or H2/Pd) installs the N-cyclohexyl group and furnishes the target O=C1CO[C@H]2CCCC[C@@H]2N1C1CCN(C2CCC(COCC(F)F)CC2)CC1.

question_text:
Propose a multi-step synthesis route for the target molecule.

Use the structured benchmark context below as the full task specification.
Return the best chemistry answer grounded only in this context.

<benchmark_context>
{
  "level": "C",
  "task_family": "Multi-Step Synthesis Planning",
  "task_subtype": "reference_route_planning",
  "difficulty": "hard",
  "coverage_tags": [
    "route_depth",
    "branch_handling",
    "convergence",
    "reference_route"
  ],
  "input": {
    "target": "C1CCN(CC2CC3(CCN2)OCCO3)C1"
  }
}
</benchmark_context>
student_answer: Step 1: Protect 4-piperidone (e.g., 4-piperidone·HCl monohydrate) as the N-Boc derivative, then ketalize the C4 ketone with ethylene glycol/p-TsOH to give N-Boc-4,4-ethylenedioxy(piperidine) (the spiro-1,3-dioxolane piperidine core). | Step 2: Form the C2 enolate of this N-Boc spiro ketal (LDA or LiHMDS) and trap with formaldehyde to install a hydroxymethyl group, giving N-Boc-2-(hydroxymethyl)-4,4-ethylenedioxy(piperidine). | Step 3: Convert the alcohol into a good leaving group (e.g., MsCl/Et3N then LiBr, or PBr3) to obtain N-Boc-2-(bromomethyl)-4,4-ethylenedioxy(piperidine). | Step 4: Displace the bromide with piperidine (SN2) to form N-Boc-2-(piperidin-1-ylmethyl)-4,4-ethylenedioxy(piperidine). | Step 5: Remove the Boc group (TFA or HCl/dioxane) to furnish C1CCN(CC2CC3(CCN2)OCCO3)C1.
rescue_original_error: OpenShell worker failed with exit_code=124: unknown error

question_text:
Propose a multi-step synthesis route for the target molecule.

Use the structured benchmark context below as the full task specification.
Return the best chemistry answer grounded only in this context.

<benchmark_context>
{
  "level": "C",
  "task_family": "Multi-Step Synthesis Planning",
  "task_subtype": "reference_route_planning",
  "difficulty": "medium",
  "coverage_tags": [
    "route_depth",
    "branch_handling",
    "convergence",
    "reference_route"
  ],
  "input": {
    "target": "COc1cc(C(C)(C)c2cnc(SCc3c(F)cc(N)cc3F)n2-c2ccc(F)cc2)ccc1Cl"
  }
}
</benchmark_context>
student_answer: Step 1: N-arylate 4-bromo-2-mercapto-1H-imidazole (or its thione tautomer; protect SH as thioacetate if needed) with 1-bromo-4-fluorobenzene to give 4-bromo-1-(4-fluorophenyl)-2-mercaptoimidazole | Step 2: Do Br/Li exchange on the 4-bromo heterocycle and trap with acetone to form 4-(2-hydroxypropan-2-yl)-1-(4-fluorophenyl)-2-mercaptoimidazole | Step 3: Friedel–Crafts alkylation of 1-chloro-3-methoxybenzene with that tertiary alcohol (Lewis acid or strong Brønsted acid) to install the diaryl tert-carbon, giving 4-[2-(4-chloro-2-methoxyphenyl)propan-2-yl]-1-(4-fluorophenyl)-2-mercaptoimidazole | Step 4: In parallel, convert 2,4-difluoro-5-nitrotoluene to 2,4-difluoro-5-nitrobenzyl bromide by benzylic bromination (NBS, hν), then reduce NO2 to NH2 to obtain 2,4-difluoro-5-aminobenzyl bromide | Step 5: Final S-alkylation of the mercapto-imidazole from Step 3 with 2,4-difluoro-5-aminobenzyl bromide furnishes COc1cc(C(C)(C)c2cnc(SCc3c(F)cc(N)cc3F)n2-c2ccc(F)cc2)ccc1Cl.
